title: rosa × schleicheri var. tenella
intro: Rosa × schleicheri var. tenella, also known as Rosa tenella, is a fragrant, deciduous rose species native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. It is used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the essential oils and flavonoids present in its leaves and flowers. Rosa tenella has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and wounds, thanks to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. The plant's medicinal properties can be utilized through herbal preparations such as infusions, teas, and topical applications, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
